rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issue 1 (negligence) are continued to April 26, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard after defendant¿s motion for a protective order regarding the plaintiffs¿ notice to depose defendant¿s PMK and COR and defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 2-5 (strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages) . If defendant succeeds in obtaining a protective order precluding the deposition of its PMK and COR, then the tentative ruling is to grant defendant¿s motion for summary judgment on the grounds that defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain admissible evidence that defendant knew or should have known that its disturbance of asbestos-containing materials created a safety hazard for a member of Russell Utterback¿s household. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen are sustained. If defendant is not successful in obtaining a protective order precluding the deposition of its PMK and COR, then the tentative ruling is to grant plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h) to a date after the completion of the deposition of defendant¿s PMK and/or COR. The foregoing tentative rulings are based on the assumption but not a formal decision that California law recognizes a cause of action sounding in negligence against a premises owner by a family member of an employee of an independent contractor of the premises owner based on ¿take-home¿ exposure to asbestos-containing dust. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages. = (220/HEK) Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿S Memorandum Of Costs

Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿s memorandum of costs is continued to April 13, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 at the request of the moving party. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.S Motion To Abate
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co. and Hanson Permanente Cement¿s motion to abate is continued to May 5 to enable plaintiff to file a motion to amend and have it noticed for May 5. The court previously continued this motion so that it would be heard the same day as plaintiff¿s motion to amend, which purportedly was noticed for April 6. However, although it appears that plaintiff¿s motion to amend was served, the motion was never filed, and therefore is not on calendar. Assuming plaintiffs will properly file their motion to be heard on May 5, defendants¿ motion to abate will be heard on the same day. = (220/HEK)
